author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Technical debt chunk 26: explicit null returns for nullable methods (#24794)

* Return null explicitly where a nullable-return method fell through

These methods declare a nullable return type (string|null, ...|null, etc.)
but reached the end of a code path with a bare `return;` or no return
statement, which PHPStan flagged. Replace the bare returns / add an explicit
`return null;` so the declared contract is met. Behaviour is unchanged — an
implicit/bare return already yields null at runtime.

Affects Filesystem::getFileSize, LogTablesProvider::getLogTable,
WidgetsProvider::factory, SessionAuth::getLogin/getName/getTokenAuthSecret,
Settings::getSetting, Mysqli/Pdo\Mysql::beginTransaction,
ViewDataTable\Factory::getReport, Visualization\Sparkline::render,
Live GetLastVisits::buildReportMetadata and SitesManager::getTimezoneFromWebsite.

* Remove obsolete phpstan-baseline entries for explicit null returns

Drops the 17 'empty return statement found' / 'return statement is missing'
entries the preceding change resolved (confirmed by a full phpstan run).

author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Ignore Twig CVE-2026-49981 in composer audit (#24804)

The advisory is a sandbox-only allow-list bypass on cached templates
when the sandbox state changes between renders. Matomo does not enable
Twig's sandbox (no SandboxExtension is registered), so it is not
exploitable, and upgrading is blocked while Matomo still supports PHP 7.2.

author: Stefan Giehl
message: Move submodule markers back to 5.x-dev (#24791)

author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Require password confirmation when granting the admin role (#24775)

* Require password confirmation when granting the admin role

Extends the existing session-scope re-authentication requirement (super user
access, anonymous view access) to also cover granting the admin role. The check
in UsersManager\API::setUserAccess() enforces it for both the string and array
forms of the access argument, and only within the api session scope so
token_auth automation and CLI keep working.

Registers the new UsersManager_AdminUserRoleChangeWarning translation key for
client-side use.

* Prompt for password confirmation when granting admin in the UI

Routes admin-role grants through the shared PasswordConfirmation modal on both
screens that can grant admin: the users list role dropdown and bulk action
(PagedUsersList), and the per-user permissions editor including give-access-to-
all-sites (UserPermissionsEdit), which previously had no password path.

* Add tests for admin-role password confirmation

Integration tests cover granting admin in the session scope without/with wrong/
with correct password, the array access form, the non-session path, and a
regression guard that non-admin grants in the session scope still need no
password. UI tests exercise the password prompt on the users list and permissions
editor admin-grant flows.

* Fix UsersManager UI tests for admin password confirmation

Update the bulk_set_access_confirm expected screenshot to the password
confirmation modal now shown for admin grants, and move the mouse away before
the single-site permission screenshot so the closed password modal does not
leave the changed row in a hover state.

* Add tests for admin grant via array access and give-access-to-all-sites

Adds a backend integration test asserting the array access form
([Admin, capability]) with the correct password succeeds in the session scope,
and a UI test covering the give-access-to-all-websites admin flow through the
password confirmation modal.

* Make all-websites admin UI test non-perturbing

Verify the give-access-to-all-websites admin flow opens the password
confirmation modal (rather than the plain confirm modal) and abort it, so the
test asserts the new branch without changing the user's access or disturbing the
following screenshot tests.

author: Thomas ZILLIOX
message:
Fix flaky Dashboard_dashboard5 screenshot via comparison threshold (#24795)

A report label in the Region widget sits on the CSS text-overflow:ellipsis
truncation boundary. Headless Chrome sub-pixel text layout is not fully
deterministic, so the label occasionally truncates one character earlier
("Fr…" vs "Fra…"), which the zero-tolerance comparison rejects. Allow a tiny
comparisonThreshold, matching how other UI specs handle this variance.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Docblock quality chunk 25: mark nullable/widened property @var types (#24790)

* Mark Access $login/$token_auth @var nullable

Both properties default to null and are guarded (isset()), so string|null.

* Mark ArchiveInvalidator $segmentArchiving @var nullable

The property is assigned null in the constructor and lazily instantiated
(guarded with empty()), so SegmentArchiving|null.

* Correct ArchiveProcessor $archive @var to ArchiveQuery|null

The property defaults to null and is assigned an ArchiveQuery (Archive::build()
returns the ArchiveQuery interface), so the concrete Archive|non-null type was wrong.

* Normalize ArchiveFilter empty restrictToDateRange to false (Date[]|false)

getRestrictToDateRange() already documents the contract as Date[]|false, but
the setter stored the raw empty input in the empty() branch, so an empty-string
argument was kept as a string. Store false there instead, so the property is
only ever a parsed Date[] or false, and correct @var to Date[]|false to match.
All reads guard with !empty() before array access, so this is behaviour-preserving.

* Mark QueueConsumer $idSite @var nullable

The property is explicitly assigned null, so int|null.

* Mark LogTablesProvider $tablesCache @var nullable

The cache is assigned null as its uninitialised sentinel (guarded with
isset()), so LogTable[]|null.

* Mark Tracker $db @var nullable

The static property defaults to null and is reset to null on disconnect,
so Db|null.

* Mark Pdo\Mysql $connection @var nullable

The connection defaults to null and is reset to null on disconnect, so PDO|null.

* Mark Visit $visitProperties @var nullable

The property is assigned null in the constructor before a VisitProperties
instance is set, so VisitProperties|null.

* Widen VisitorRecognizer $visitRow @var to array|false

false is assigned as the legitimate no-matching-visitor sentinel.

* Remove obsolete phpstan-baseline entries for nullable property docblocks

Drops the 18 entries the preceding @var corrections made obsolete, including
the now-justified is_null()/empty() checks that were previously flagged as
pointless (confirmed by a full phpstan run reporting them as no-longer-matched).

author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Docblock quality chunk 24: correct stale @return/@param/@var types (#24784)

* Correct getDirectoryParentFromList @return to string|null

The method returns null (return null;) when no parent directory is found,
so the string-only docblock was too narrow.

* Fix optimizeTables $tables @param to match native array type

The native parameter type is array, so the @param string|array docblock
was not a subtype of it; drop the string alternative.

* Correct buildCategory/buildSubcategoryMetadata @return to array|null

Both methods return null when the category/subcategory is not set.

* Fix DataComparisonFilter $request @var to \Piwik\Request

The property is assigned a Request object and used as one (getStringParameter()
etc.); the array docblock was wrong.

* Fix UserInviteEmail $invitedUser @var to array

The value is documented @param array and accessed with array keys
(['email'], ['login']); object was wrong.

* Fix FeatureFlagManager $logger @var to LoggerInterface

The constructor accepts and assigns a LoggerInterface; drop the now-unused
Logger import.

* Widen Writer $validationMessage @var to string|array|null

It is assigned $validator->getMessage(), documented @return array, so array
is a legitimate value.

* Fix $processedPercent @var to float

The value is assigned from ceil(), which returns float.

* Correct UserTableFilter $filterByRole @var to string|null

The property is assigned null and guarded with isset(); null is legitimate.

* Correct getReferrerTypeFromShortName @return to string|int

The function works in both directions and returns the numeric referrer-type
constant via array_search when given a short name.

* Correct getDateRangeToDeleteFrom @return to string[]

The method converts the Date objects to datetime strings via getDatetime()
before returning.

* Correct getDateRangeToCalculate @return to string[]

The method returns datetime strings (getDatetime()), never Date objects.

* Correct ServerModule getLocation/isWorking return docblocks

getLocation() returns false when no fallback resolves the location, and
isWorking() returns a translated error string when the server var is missing.

* Correct roundRevenueIfNeeded @return to int|float|false

The method returns false when the passed revenue is false.

* Remove obsolete phpstan-baseline entries for corrected docblocks

Drops the 22 entries the preceding docblock corrections made obsolete
(confirmed by a full phpstan run reporting them as no-longer-matched).

author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Docblock quality chunk 23: fix structurally broken PHPDoc annotations (#24783)

* Fill empty @return tags on Tracker Db proxy methods

fetchRow() and exec() are proxies to fetch() and had empty @return tags
that PHPStan could not parse; fetch() returns a fetched row (assoc array)
or false, so document @return array|false.

* Remove orphaned @var docblock in PHPBinaryCheck

A stray '@var int' block with no variable name sat above the constructor,
documenting no property; remove it.

* Remove stale @var for non-existent variable in Marketplace config

The Service factory closure carried '@var Service $previous', but there
is no $previous variable in the closure; remove the orphaned annotation.

* Fix stale ProcessedMetric import and misplaced closure @var in Graph

The @var for $extraProcessedMetrics referenced a non-existent
Piwik\Plugins\AbTesting\...\ProcessedMetric; point the import at the real
core Piwik\Plugin\ProcessedMetric. The '@var DataTable $dataTable' sat
directly above a foreach so PHPStan tied it to the loop variable; type the
closure parameter natively instead (filter() always passes a DataTable).

* Remove obsolete phpstan-baseline entries for the fixed annotations

Drops the nine entries the preceding docblock fixes made obsolete: the two
@return-invalid-value entries (Tracker/Db.php), the misplaced/nameless @var
entries (PHPBinaryCheck, Marketplace config), and the Graph.php entries that
resolved once the ProcessedMetric import and closure parameter type were
corrected.

* Fix invalid 'callback' type in DataTable filter docblocks

The @param tags used the removed PHP 'callback' pseudo-type, which PHPStan
resolves as an unknown class Piwik\DataTable\Filter\callback. Both values
are invoked / checked with is_callable(), so the intended type is callable.

* Remove malformed empty @throws tags

Six @throws tags had no type after them and could not be parsed by PHPStan.
An empty @throws conveys nothing, so remove them (valid @throws tags in the
same files are left untouched).

* Remove obsolete phpstan-baseline entries for callback/@throws fixes

Drops the two 'has invalid type ...callback' entries and the six
'@throws has invalid value' entries the preceding fixes made obsolete.

author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Docblock quality fixes (chunk 22) (#24782)

* Fix stale getCategoryId link in Category docblock

* Fix copy-paste class summary in CategoryList

* Correct queryConversionsByDimension extraFrom note

* Include false/null in getActionUrl return docblock

* Correct $errorCodesToIgnore @var type in Sql migration

* Include false in getRawBulkRequest return docblock

* Fix trackingCodeGenerator summary (returns, not echoes)

* Fix metric name in Sparklines class docblock example

* Correct by-reference marker on $visits event param docblocks

* Fix stale unit-test @see reference in SearchEngine

* Correct UserAccessFilter class summary (write access, same site)

* Fix wrong class reference in InvalidateReportData segment docblocks

getAllSegments() returns associative DB-row arrays from SegmentEditor
API::getAll() (accessed via $segment['idsegment'], ['definition'], etc.),
not Piwik\Segment objects, so array<Segment> was incorrect.

* Fix PasswordResetter email-from property @var types

$emailFromName and $emailFromAddress are assigned only from nullable
constructor parameters with no non-null default, so both are string|null.
$emailFromAddress had an empty (malformed) @var; drop the now-obsolete
baseline entry it required.

* Fix malformed inline @var annotations in ControllerResolver and Login Model

ControllerResolver::createPluginController had a reversed inline @var
($controller Controller) that phpstan could not parse; corrected to the
resolvable \Piwik\Plugin\Controller type. Login Model::$tablePrefixed
had an empty @var; it is assigned Common::prefixTable() (string). Drops
the two now-obsolete baseline entries.

* Fix reversed inline @var annotations in Map and Period

Both had inline annotations with the type and variable name swapped
($subDataTableMap Map / $currentPeriod Period) that phpstan could not
parse; reorder to the valid @var <type> <$var> form. Drops the last
three @var-invalid-value baseline entries.

* Fix malformed @param annotations in UsersManager Tasks and Urls setting

Tasks::$usersManagerApi is a property, so its @param tag should be @var API.
Urls::cleanParameterUrls() had @param string|array urls missing the $ on
the variable name. Both were unparseable by PHPStan; drops the two obsolete
baseline entries.

* Fix malformed @param annotations in Password, Option and Segment

Password::info() and Option::setSingletonInstance() had @param tags with
no variable name; add the missing $hash / $instance. Segment::getSelectQuery()
carried an orphaned @param (no variable name) whose text duplicated the
already-documented $limit parameter; remove it. Drops the three obsolete
baseline entries.

author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Escape query-string characters in the segment value of AJAX request URLs (#24770)

* Escape query-string characters in the segment value of AJAX request URLs

The segment parameter is appended to the request URL without running it
through a full encode helper, because some legacy callers pass an already
pre-encoded value and encoding again would double-encode it. As a result an
unencoded segment value could contain characters with query-string meaning
that changed how the resulting URL was parsed.

Manually escape those characters so the segment always stays a single
parameter. A correctly encoded value never contains them literally, so
already-encoded segments are left untouched. Adds AjaxHelper tests and
rebuilds the CoreHome Vue bundle.

* Strengthen AjaxHelper segment encoding tests

Cover the '?' escaping branch and add a realistic segment value to lock in
that segment operators ('==') and inner '=' separators are preserved while
query-string characters are escaped.

* Present the UI-test browser as regular Chrome so tracking is not blocked

The TrackingSpamPrevention plugin blocks tracking requests from headless
browsers by default (block_headless), matching a HeadlessChrome user
agent or Sec-CH-UA brand. Puppeteer's browser reports exactly that, so
any tracking that originates from the test browser - the JS tracker, and
server-side requests that inherit the browser user agent - was excluded
and produced no visits/failures. Override the user agent and client-hint
brands on each page so the test browser presents as a regular Chrome.

* update expected test files

---------

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>
Co-authored-by: dependabot[bot] <49699333+dependabot[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sgiehl <stefan@matomo.org>

author: Stefan Giehl
message:
Resolve user logins to their stored form in UsersManager checks (#24769)

* Resolve user logins to their stored form in UsersManager checks

Logins are matched case- and accent-insensitively by the database
collation, so different representations of a login can refer to the same
stored user. Add a getCanonicalLogin() helper and use it in
setUserAccess(), addCapabilities() and checkUserIsNotAnonymous() so these
checks operate on the actual stored login instead of the raw request
value. Add regression tests covering these paths.

* apply review feedback

* Canonicalize login in setUserPreference()

Align setUserPreference() with the other UsersManager checks so the
reserved anonymous user is recognised regardless of its representation
and the preference is stored under the login as stored in the database
rather than the raw request value.

author: caddoo
message:
Auto-retry flaky preview builds up to 3 times before notifying Slack (#24754)

* Auto-retry flaky preview builds up to 3 times before notifying Slack

The nightly preview build currently fires a Slack alert on every failed
run, and an engineer has to hand-rerun the job. Given that most of these
failures are flaky UI/system tests, and the CI/UI suite will be reworked
for Matomo 6 anyway, absorb up to two flaky retries before paging humans.

- Remove the inline notify_slack_on_failure job from release-preview.yml.
- Add preview-retry-handler.yml: a workflow_run listener on "Build
  preview release" that reruns failed jobs on attempts 1 and 2 via
  `gh run rerun --failed`, and only POSTs to Slack once run_attempt
  reaches 3. The handler has to be a separate workflow because GitHub
  refuses to rerun a workflow while any of its jobs are still running;
  a workflow_run: completed listener sees the source run as complete.
- The handler only acts on scheduled runs that ended in failure, so
  manual dispatches of the preview build are unaffected.

* Address review findings on preview retry handler

- Notify Slack even when the retry step itself fails: without this,
  a broken `gh run rerun` (rate limit, permission edge, network)
  would silently leave the preview build unreleased for the day.
- Include the handler run URL in the "retry mechanism failed" Slack
  payload so on-call can jump straight to the retry-step logs.
- Add `timeout-minutes: 5` on the handler job so a stuck `gh` call
  doesn't idle a runner for 6 hours.
- Move the "notifications live in preview-retry-handler.yml" pointer
  from a dangling comment inside the jobs list to the top-of-file
  comment block, where it reads as intended.
- Rework Slack payload wording ("three consecutive attempts") and
  drop the trailing empty line on the final-attempt branch.
